title: Trading System Implementation
description: Detailed guide for building a real-time player-to-player trading system with escrow, atomic transaction processing, validation, anti-fraud measures, and marketplace integration. Covers direct trades and global marketplace listings.
anti_patterns: client-side trade logic that can be tampered with
no escrow mechanism allowing players to back out after confirmation
skip validation of item ownership and trade eligibility
non-atomic settlement that can result in item duplication or loss
no rate limiting enabling automated farming
ignoring value disparity in trades facilitating real-money trading
no audit logging for dispute resolution
trusting client-submitted trade state without server verification
implementation_steps: Build the Trade Gateway as a WebSocket-based entry point managing real-time trade sessions with unique IDs and defined lifecycle states: initiated, proposed, confirmed, executing, completed, cancelled

Implement the Escrow Service to hold items and currency in a neutral server-side account during trade; remove items from player inventory and place in escrow before trade finalization to prevent either party from backing out after confirmation

Build the Validation Engine to verify trade eligibility including item ownership verification, trade lock checks, account restriction validation, and inventory capacity checks; run synchronously before any escrow operation
Implement the Settlement Engine to perform atomic swap of escrowed items between players using database transactions with row-level locking to guarantee consistency
Create the Audit Logger to record every trade event with full context (session ID, event type, player ID, details JSON, timestamp) for dispute resolution and fraud analysis

Implement the trade lifecycle: Initiation (Player A invites Player B, gateway creates session) → Proposal (both add items/currency, real-time broadcast) → Lock-in (each confirms their side, no modifications after confirmation) → Escrow Transfer (move all offered items to escrow, rollback if any transfer fails) → Settlement (distribute escrowed items atomically) → Completion (both receive items and trade receipt, session archived)

Design the database schema: trade_sessions (id, player_a_id, player_b_id, status, timestamps), trade_offers (id, session_id, player_id, item_id, quantity, currency_amount, confirmed), escrow_entries (id, session_id, player_id, item_id, quantity, state: pending/held/released/returned), trade_audit_log (id, session_id, event_type, player_id, details_json, timestamp)
Ensure all mutations occur within serializable database transactions to prevent race conditions like double-spending items across simultaneous trades

Build marketplace integration: listings (item + price, active until fulfilled or expired after 7 days), search/filter (indexed by item type, rarity, price, seller with full-text search), instant buy (atomic escrow-and-settle flow), optional auction/bidding (highest bid at close triggers settlement)

Implement anti-fraud measures: rate limiting (50 trades/hour, 10 concurrent sessions), value disparity detection (flag trades exceeding 10:1 ratio based on marketplace historical prices), new account restrictions (under 24h old or less than 5h playtime cannot trade high-value items), trade bans (moderator-issued), duplicate item detection (verify item provenance chains before escrow)

Implement error handling: 60-second reconnection window for disconnected players before trade cancellation and escrow return; overflow slots with 24-hour claim window for inventory-full situations; full rollback on any escrow-phase error to prevent item loss or duplication
rules: Server-side escrow is mandatory: items must be held in escrow before trade finalization to prevent either party from backing out after confirmation
All trade mutations must use serializable database transactions to prevent race conditions and double-spending
The Validation Engine must run synchronously before any escrow operation to verify item ownership, trade locks, account restrictions, and inventory capacity
Settlement must be atomic: the swap of escrowed items between players must succeed or fail as a whole unit
Audit logging is mandatory: every trade event must be recorded with full context for dispute resolution and fraud analysis
Rate limit trading activity: maximum 50 trades per hour and 10 concurrent trade sessions per player to prevent automated farming
Flag trades with value disparity exceeding 10:1 ratio for review based on marketplace historical prices
New accounts (under 24 hours old or fewer than 5 hours playtime) cannot initiate trades with high-value items
If a player disconnects during a trade, maintain the session for 60 seconds before cancellation and escrow return
If settlement fails due to inventory full, hold items in temporary overflow with 24-hour claim window
All errors during the escrow phase must trigger a full rollback to ensure no items are lost or duplicated
Duplicate item detection: verify item provenance chains before accepting items into escrow

examples.name: Trade Lifecycle Flow
examples.description: Player A invites Player B → both add items and currency → both lock-in their offers → items moved to escrow → atomic settlement distributes items → both receive new items and trade receipt

examples.name: Marketplace Instant Buy
examples.description: Buyer purchases a listing which triggers an atomic escrow-and-settle flow identical to direct trade but without the proposal step, ensuring the same safety guarantees

examples.name: Anti-Fraud: Value Disparity Detection
examples.description: A trade where Player A offers items worth 1000 Gold and Player B offers items worth 50 Gold (20:1 ratio) is flagged for review since it exceeds the 10:1 threshold

examples.name: Error Handling: Disconnection Recovery
examples.description: Player disconnects mid-trade; session remains open for 60 seconds; if player reconnects, trade resumes; if not, trade is cancelled and all escrowed items are returned to original owners

examples.name: Database Schema: Serializable Transactions
examples.description: All mutations to trade_sessions, trade_offers, and escrow_entries tables occur within serializable database transactions to prevent double-spending an item across simultaneous trades
